wrong_choice_explanations: が is a subject marker for new information or emphasis — it doesn't fit here because we're simply stating what the topic (私) is.
を marks direct objects of verbs — it doesn't fit here because 学生 is not an object being acted upon.
に indicates direction, time, or indirect location — it doesn't fit here because we're not expressing movement or time.
が emphasizes the subject as new information — it doesn't fit here because we're talking about today as a known topic.
を marks direct objects — it doesn't fit here because 雨 is not being acted upon by a verb.
で indicates location of action or means — it doesn't fit here because we're describing the weather, not an action location.
が marks the subject for emphasis or new information — it doesn't fit here because we're commenting on この本 as the topic.
を marks direct objects of transitive verbs — it doesn't fit here because 面白い is an adjective, not a transitive verb.
と means 'with' or 'and' — it doesn't fit here because we're not expressing accompaniment or listing.
が emphasizes the subject as new or surprising information — it doesn't fit here because we're simply identifying what Tanaka-san is.
に indicates direction, time, or location — it doesn't fit here because we're not expressing movement or position.
から means 'from' or 'because' — it doesn't fit here because we're not indicating origin or reason.
が marks what is liked in preference expressions — it doesn't fit here because 映画 is the topic we're commenting about.
を marks direct objects — it doesn't fit here because 好き is an adjective expressing preference, not a transitive verb.
の shows possession or modification — it doesn't fit here because we're not showing ownership or describing something.
が emphasizes the subject — it doesn't fit here because we're making a general statement about cats as a topic.
を marks direct objects of verbs — it doesn't fit here because かわいい is an adjective, not a transitive verb.
で indicates location of action or means — it doesn't fit here because we're describing a quality, not an action or method.
が marks the subject for emphasis — it doesn't fit here because we're commenting on Japanese as a general topic.
に indicates direction, time, or location — it doesn't fit here because we're describing a characteristic, not position or time.
と means 'with' or 'and' — it doesn't fit here because we're not expressing accompaniment or listing items.
が emphasizes new information about the subject — it doesn't fit here because we're stating what tomorrow is as a topic.
を marks direct objects — it doesn't fit here because 休み is not being acted upon by a verb.
から means 'from' or 'because' — it doesn't fit here because we're not indicating origin or starting point.
が emphasizes the subject — it doesn't fit here because we're describing the coffee as a topic we're commenting on.
を marks direct objects of transitive verbs — it doesn't fit here because 熱い is an adjective, not a transitive verb.
で indicates location of action or means — it doesn't fit here because we're describing temperature, not where an action occurs.

exercises.translation: My mother is good at cooking.
wrong_choice_explanations: が marks the subject — it doesn't fit here because 母 is the topic we're commenting about, not the subject being emphasized.
を marks direct objects — it doesn't fit here because 母 is not being acted upon by a verb.
に indicates direction or location — it doesn't fit here because we're not expressing movement toward or position of the mother.
